source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
On her way to school Maria first had to run to the underground, she exited from that after two stops and subsequently walked the rest of the way by foot all the way to school. Which of the following speed-time-diagrams best describes her journey to school?
<image1>
extraction_notes: options field contains only option labels without option text/diagram content, so answer is kept as the original label

image_dependency: Question asks which speed-time diagram matches the journey, so the candidate diagrams must be inspected visually.
Choices are only A-E labels; the actual option content is not present in text and is therefore image-based.
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and image_manifest shows 1 existing PNG image.
multi_step: The journey description contains multiple sequential phases: run to the underground, travel on the underground for two stops, then walk to school.
Selecting the correct diagram requires aligning several time segments and relative speeds with a visual speed-time representation.
This is multimodal reasoning: textual journey description must be matched against image-based diagrams.
Multiple-choice format with 5 options supports nontrivial comparison across alternatives.
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice and answer_text is a single explicit label: D.
A downstream checker can verify whether option D's diagram matches the stated sequence of motion segments.
asset_registry_record shows answer_present=true and registry_passed=true, so the core assets needed for checking are available.
The target representation is structured (speed-time diagram), which is more rule-checkable than an open-ended free-response answer.

decision_reasons: Asset registry passed with core assets present: question text, one existing image, and an answer.
Although the option content is only in the image and the answer is a single choice label, the required image asset is available, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Initial scoring indicates very high image dependency and strong verifiability, making this a useful multimodal candidate.
The problem appears to require multi-step alignment between the textual journey description and visual speed-time diagrams, supporting candidate value.

agent_assessment.rationale: The text clearly describes a journey with three phases (run, underground with two stops, then walk), and the image provides readable speed-time diagram options A-E needed to answer the question. The expected answer format is a single option letter, and the visual choices are semantically interpretable.
